findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 41. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The PDF uses a three-column layout with time slots on the left, agenda items in the center, and presenter names on the right. The HTML collapses this into a single-column layout where presenters are placed inline in parentheses after the agenda item title rather than in a separate right-aligned column.
findings.evidence: PDF: Time, Agenda Topic, and Presenter are visually separated into three distinct columns (e.g., '10:25-10:45  Discuss Model Schematic' on the left/center with '(Steve Nebiker and Casey Caldwell)' right-aligned). HTML: These are merged into a single line like '10:25-10:45 Discuss Model Schematic (Steve Nebiker and Casey Caldwell)'.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements create invalid HTML structure. The document has a <main> inside another <main>, which is semantically incorrect.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> wrapping the entire content, then another <main> wrapping the agenda sections inside it.
